findings.finding: Joint-based deformation with von-Mises weighting works correctly for rendering. No hole artifact. Bend produces visible curvature.

formula.f: exp(c * dot(dir, bump_dir)) / exp(c)  — von-Mises weight, 1 at peak
formula.lever: dir * amplitude * f  — arm from joint to surface point, along dir
formula.angle: lean_amount + bend_amount * f  — lean: constant; bend: von-Mises weighted
formula.contribution: rotate_axis_angle(lever, lean_axis, angle)  — rotated lever added to joint
formula.pos: dir * rmin + sum of contributions over all bumps

findings.normals: finite difference on eval_pos, 4 neighbour samples, pole guard for degenerate dphi
findings.billboards: camera-facing for sharpness, surface normal passed through for lighting
findings.normal_flip: if dot(normal, view_dir) < 0: normal = -normal — handles concave regions
findings.note: lean has slightly more compression than pure rigid tilt due to longer lever arm at peak — acceptable

findings.finding: Alternative ghost-sphere rendering formulation was explored but not validated.
findings.note: May produce cleaner bend geometry. Not tested. Do not use until validated against working approach.

findings.finding: Ghost point for collision is the center of a star-shaped coordinate system covering the deformed bump region.
findings.status: validated visually — marker confirmed correct position

formula.interpretation: joint minus rmin in direction of rotated lever — behind the bump tip

findings.constraint: ghost must remain within rmin sphere for star-shape guarantee from ghost center
findings.max_bend_lean: see lean_bend_constraints entry — replaced by tangent condition
findings.negative_radius: when ghost outside rmin sphere, inverted ray gives negative r — model handles this but failure states possible at extremes

findings.finding: Collision between two blobs detected via radius field intersection. No explicit geometric intersection needed.
pipeline: 1. For each pair of blobs: find directions dir where r_A(dir) overlaps r_B(dir_transformed) — intersection ring on sphere
2. Intersection ring center = contact point, normal = contact normal
3. Place ghost point per object on contact normal, distance proportional to penetration depth
4. Apply Von-Mises minus Gaussian deformation field from ghost point onto existing surface
5. Adjust A parameter (flatness) until surfaces no longer overlap — stiffness controls rate
findings.intersection_ring: numerical search on radius field — 1D curve on sphere where both fields equal
findings.ghost_per_collision: one ghost point per collision pair per object — scales linearly with collision count
findings.asymmetric_contacts: ghost on actual contact normal handles side-of-bump and valley contacts automatically

findings.finding: Von-Mises minus Gaussian composition produces smooth flat-topped lobe suitable for contact deformation and sensing.
findings.status: design — formula confirmed, not yet implemented
findings.flatness_condition: A_opt = cv*exp(cv) / (2*cg) — zeroes second derivative at peak

behavior.A_below_opt: flat zone present, width increases as A decreases from A_opt
behavior.A_at_opt: flattest top, collapses to point contact at A_opt
behavior.A_above_opt: ring bump above 1 — useful for sensing contact boundary

parameters.cg: controls Gaussian sharpness — higher cg = broader subtraction
parameters.A: controls flat zone size, bounded by A_opt

findings.practical_values: cv=5.1 cg=7.3 A~11.5 gives ~10-11 degree flat zone
findings.multi_term: complex contacts (bump-in-valley, blob-blob, asymmetric) require sum of basis terms with independent centers, widths, weights

findings.finding: Deformation field and volume rescale must be solved jointly as a constraint.
pipeline: 1. Ghost sphere detects collision via radius field, produces penetration depth and contact normal
2. Deformation field parameterized by contact geometry (normal, patch size, stiffness ratio)
3. Solve (deformation params, scale s) jointly such that deformed+scaled surface is tangent to contact surface
4. Apply deformation and scale, compute repulsive force from penetration depth and displaced volume
findings.displaced_volume: spherical cap: V_cap = pi * h^2 * (r - h/3)

volume_rescale.rule: rescale only non-contact hemisphere to avoid re-penetration

findings.kink_avoidance: spatially-varying scale field using same Von-Mises/Gaussian basis
findings.non_sensory_note: approximate conformance acceptable for non-sensory blobs

findings.finding: Valley between bumps is concave on receiving surface — deformation is inward, not outward.
findings.basis_handling: same Von-Mises/Gaussian basis applies with negative amplitude
findings.multi_term_needed: when bump tip hits valley slope contact is asymmetric and off-center — single lobe insufficient
findings.ghost_sphere: handles repulsion correctly in all cases via transformed frame regardless of surface concavity

findings.finding: Deformation field doubles as sensory surface — conformance quality directly determines sensing fidelity.
findings.pure_von_mises_limit: cannot produce flat contact patch — nonzero curvature at peak always present
findings.solution: Von-Mises minus Gaussian approximation sufficient — exact flatness not required
findings.sensory_blobs: each basis term is a detectable feature: contact center, boundary ring, secondary lobes — coefficients are the sensory signal
findings.non_sensory_blobs: close conformance acceptable, volume conservation and repulsion are primary requirements

findings.finding: Motor layer is prescribed oscillation with hierarchical control. Nervous system modulates oscillation, does not compute raw targets.

structure.movement_set: Collection of bumps with fixed relative phases, frequencies, amplitude ratios. Multiple sets can run simultaneously at partial amplitude.
structure.global_amplitude: One scalar per set. Primary control surface — driving to zero silences the set.
structure.parameter_modulation: Individual bump parameters within a set (direction, frequency, amplitude ratio) can be nudged independently — handles steering and obstacle navigation.
structure.additivity: Sets are additive. Gait, reach, recoil etc can coexist at partial amplitude. Incoherent superpositions are an evolutionary concern, not a hard constraint.

evolution.timescale_slow: Oscillation patterns themselves — frequencies, phases, amplitude ratios, set membership. Body morphology equivalent. Evolves at slower pace.
evolution.timescale_fast: Routing — which sensory inputs modulate which oscillation parameters at what weight. The nervous system. Evolves on top of stable motor primitives.
evolution.first_experiments: Nervous system absent. Pure oscillation evolution only. Validates motor layer before routing complexity enters.

findings.sensing_integration: Sensory basis terms (Von-Mises minus Gaussian descriptors) route into oscillation parameter modulation. Computation model between sensing and motor not yet decided — alternatives to neural networks under consideration.
findings.note: Movement sets are genome from the start, not fixed infrastructure. Two evolutionary timescales are a design choice, not an emergent property.

findings.finding: Sensory-to-motor computation via function composition graph operating in the basis language. Geometric operations on basis term descriptors, not arithmetic on raw scalars.
findings.status: design — approach selected, not yet formalized
findings.rationale: Linear map rejected as low potential — cannot combine sensory terms or respond to geometric relationships. Neural networks deferred. Function graph in basis language is evolvable, interpretable, and consistent with existing sensing and motor vocabulary.
findings.operations: Candidate operations: project contact direction onto bump axis, convolve two basis terms, scale amplitude by directional alignment. Full operation set not yet defined.
findings.evolution_target: Graph topology and parameter-to-oscillation routing. Operations themselves are fixed primitives.
findings.next: Internal states (object permanence, long-term behaviour) layer on top of reactive graph once reactive layer validated.

findings.finding: Lean and bend have separate limits derived from different geometric conditions. Bend limit uses tangent condition in 2D lean plane. Lean fixed at 60 degrees.
findings.max_lean: Fixed PI/3 (60 degrees). Concentration adjustment deferred — both lean and bend become less safe with higher concentration, but at different rates.
findings.max_bend: Bisection on tangent condition: find bend angle where a ray from ghost center becomes tangent to the deformed surface in the lean plane. Uses full shader deformation formula: pos(theta) = dir(theta)*rmin + lever(theta), lever rotated by bend*f where f is Von-Mises weight. Ghost at (-sin(bend)*rmin, rmin*(1-cos(bend))). 128 samples inner search, 24 bisection steps. Worst case: all angle is bend, lean=0.
findings.lean_bend_interaction: max_bend scaled down linearly with lean: max_bend_effective = max_bend * (1 - lean_fraction * 0.5). At max lean, bend is halved. Lean fraction computed after lean clamp.
findings.concentration_effect: Both lean and bend become less safe with higher concentration. Bend strongly sensitive, lean slightly sensitive. Single shared budget was wrong — they have different geometric effects.
findings.ghost_magnitude_check: Kept as helper in blob.rs (ghost_magnitude fn), currently unused in constraint. Ghost-inside-rmin is too conservative as a standalone constraint — ghost exits rmin well before star-shape breaks.
findings.note: Tangent-from-ghost approach only works when full deformation formula is used consistently for both surface positions and ghost position. Earlier attempts with simplified surface curve failed silently — bisection returned PI/2 unconditionally.
